name: addText
description: Place a typographic fragment on the scene. Use short phrases (1–8 words) that carry meaning without being explanatory. Text is well-suited to testimonial, sustained, low-onset-density moments. Returns the new element_id.

content.description: The text to place. Short fragment, 1–8 words. Examples: "after", "the encampment", "what remains".

style.description: Free-form style hint. Examples: "serif, large", "light weight, small", "uppercase, tight tracking". The renderer interprets this.

lifetime_s.description: Optional. Omit this field and the text persists for the entire performance — this is almost always what you want, because testimony accumulates. Supply an explicit number ONLY for deliberately ephemeral text (a passing interjection the scene should not keep). There is no implicit timeout; silence at this field means permanence.

reactivity.description: Optional audio-reactive bindings. See hijaz_base.md §Reactivity. Each entry binds one DOM property to one audio feature with a configurable map + easing. Favor a handful of load-bearing reactive elements over a scene where everything moves.

layer.description: Optional depth band. Omit for the per-type default (text defaults to foreground). See hijaz_base.md §Motion presets for guidance.

motion.description: Optional motion preset. See hijaz_base.md §Motion presets. Composes with any explicit reactivity bindings.

name: addSVG
description: Place a symbolic drawn form on the scene. You generate real inline SVG markup for the renderer to place. Use forms when the music has structural events (aug2 leap, tahwil, phrase climax), and keep them inside the signature visual grammar: threshold or doorframe, manuscript line, stone plane, lamp glow, textile edge, held architectural span. Returns the new element_id.

svg_markup.description: Valid, self-contained inline SVG markup. Include an <svg> root with a viewBox. The drawing may be small, band-scale, or fullscreen; it may use layered paths, gradients, masks, clip paths, local symbols/use references, and SVG filter effects when the music asks for weight or density. Slow inline SVG animation is allowed when musically motivated: cycles of at least 4 seconds, one or two animated attributes, no decorative motion. No scripts, no external assets, no raster embeds, no foreignObject, and no event handlers. Favor clear inline attributes.

position.description: Free-form position string. Examples: "lower-right", "center", "left edge", "horizontal band at mid-height".

lifetime_s.description: Optional. Omit this field and the SVG is given a default 35-second presence so it has time to be read. Supply an explicit longer value (60–120s) for forms that should anchor a passage — a sustained horizon, a held column, an atmospheric wash. Supply a shorter value only for fast, deliberately transient gestures like an angular break that the scene is meant to forget.

semantic_label.description: Short human-readable phrase (3–6 words) describing what the SVG is compositionally. Required for scene-state memory across cycles. Examples: "thin vertical line left", "angular break lower-right", "slow horizontal band".

reactivity.description: Optional audio-reactive bindings. See hijaz_base.md §Reactivity. SVG forms respond well to tahwil impulses (scale ring-outs) and hijaz_state gating. Bind the reactivity to a depicted thing, not to a geometric primitive: a candle flame whose scale surges on each tahwil (curve: impulse), trembling leaves whose rotation jumps on each onset, breath rising from a sleeping figure whose translateY drifts with hijaz_intensity — these are the Session I signature moves.

layer.description: Optional depth band. Omit for the per-type default (SVG defaults to foreground). See hijaz_base.md §Motion presets.

motion.description: Optional motion preset — breathe, pulse, orbit, drift, tremble. Composes with explicit reactivity. See hijaz_base.md §Motion presets for per-preset guidance.

name: addImage
description: Place a photographic image via search query. Photographs are the figurative heart of the scene — stone, light, a hand on a wall, a lamp, a room, a textile — what the room sits with while the music unfolds. Reach for this tool often; a scene that loses its figurative photography for long passages feels hollow. Soft floor: use addImage in most cycles, and at minimum once every three cycles. Images now have a default time-to-live (~25 s) and dissolve out over ~8 s, so new images replace old ones without needing a manual fadeElement. The query you choose IS the compositional act. Returns the new element_id.

query.description: The search query. Be deliberate — avoid generic terms like "Arabic music" or "desert". Reach for texture, place, quality: "threshold light through a doorway", "stone wall in low sun", "salt flats at dusk", "oil lamp on a stone ledge", "worn cotton textile by a window".

position.description: Free-form position string. Examples: "background", "upper-right quarter", "full bleed", "small inset, lower-left".

lifetime_s.description: Optional. Omit this field and the image fades naturally after ~25 s with an ~8 s dissolve — this is almost always what you want, because figurative turnover gives the scene its breathing quality. Pass lifetime_s: null (explicitly) to make ONE foundational image that anchors the whole performance. Pass a smaller number (e.g. 8) for a deliberate flash.

reactivity.description: Optional audio-reactive bindings. See hijaz_base.md §Reactivity. Images react well to slow features (hijaz_intensity driving color_hue drift or saturation; spectral_centroid driving slow rotation; hijaz_intensity driving a passing blur). Avoid binding fast features to images — photographic content reads as flickering under fast reactions.

layer.description: Optional depth band. Omit for the image-type default (midground). Reach for background only when you want the image deliberately behind other content.

motion.description: Optional motion preset. A `breathe` preset on a photographic anchor (a lamp, a textile, a figure) is a characteristic Session I move — the image inhales with the music's energy. See hijaz_base.md §Motion presets.

name: setBackground
description: Shift the atmospheric background. Provide a real CSS background value. Backgrounds persist indefinitely until you change them with another setBackground call — there is no automatic fade. Use rarely for performance-arc structural moves: tahwil, extended silence, a climax, final-tableau settling, or when accumulated scene density demands a new absorbing atmosphere.

css_background.description: Valid CSS background value, not prose. Examples: "radial-gradient(circle at 30% 20%, rgba(180,140,90,0.45), rgba(10,10,12,0.95) 70%)", "linear-gradient(180deg, #201814 0%, #090909 100%)".

name: fadeElement
description: Reduce or remove an existing element from the scene. Use as actively as the add tools — fading IS composition. Element IDs come from the current scene state summary. Unknown IDs return an error tool result that you will see on the next cycle. Also accepts a composition_group_id (e.g. "group_0003") to fade every member of that composition group in one call.

element_id.description: The element_id of the element to fade (e.g. "elem_0004") or the composition_group_id of a group to fade as a unit (e.g. "group_0003"). Must match an id visible in the current scene state summary.

name: addCompositeScene
description:
Place 2 to 5 related visual elements in one call, grouped as a composition. Use this when the music asks for a layered response — multiple elements entering together that belong to the same moment. Each element in the group gets its own element_id but shares a composition_group_id that links them. The group can later be faded together by calling fadeElement with the composition_group_id.

Use this tool for moments of sustained intensity, for climaxes, for tahwil arrivals where the scene needs to commit to a new register, for atmospheric foundations where a text + image + svg combination grounds a new section, or for the final tableau when existing motifs need to lock into one composed image. Do not use it for every cycle — a scene of entirely composite groups is as monotonous as a scene of entirely single elements.

Each element in the 'elements' array follows the same structure as the single-element tools: type is 'text', 'svg', or 'image'; other fields match the individual tool's required fields for that type.

content.description: For type='text': the text content. Required when type is 'text'.

svg_markup.description: For type='svg': the SVG markup. Required when type is 'svg'.

query.description: For type='image': the image search query. Required when type is 'image'.

semantic_label.description: For type='svg': short human-readable phrase. Required when type is 'svg'.

style.description: For type='text': free-form style hints. Optional.

reactivity.description: Optional audio-reactive bindings for this member. See hijaz_base.md §Reactivity. Each member's reactivity is independent — a composite can mix reactive and non-reactive members.

layer.description: Optional depth band for this member. Mix layers freely inside a composite — a background image with a foreground text fragment is the canonical shape.

motion.description: Optional motion preset for this member. See hijaz_base.md §Motion presets.

group_label.description: Short human-readable phrase (3-8 words) describing the compositional intent of this group. E.g., 'the room someone just left', 'climax response', 'threshold arrival'. Used in scene state summary so you can recognize your own composition groups across cycles.

lifetime_s.description: Lifetime in seconds for the whole group. Optional; defaults to the max default-lifetime of the element types in the group.

name: setP5Background
description: Replace the background p5 sketch layer. Runs behind all other elements. Use for ambient, atmospheric scenes — a flickering oil-lamp interior, a slowly rippling textile, calligraphic strokes forming and fading, a lantern's glow across stone. Sketches MUST depict recognizable things, namable in one sentence as a scene or object. See hijaz_base.md §Sketches for details. Only one background slot; each call replaces the prior. Returns {sketch_id, retired_id?}.

code.description: Complete p5.js sketch as JavaScript. Runs in a sandboxed iframe. Access audio features via window.features: amplitude (0..1), onset_strength (0..1), spectral_centroid (Hz), hijaz_state (enum string), hijaz_intensity (0..1), hijaz_tahwil (boolean). No external network, no parent DOM access. Depict a recognizable thing — lantern, textile, ink stroke, doorway, threshold light, hand, lamp glow, fragment of calligraphy.

audio_reactive.description: If true, window.features is populated every frame with live audio values. If false, the sketch runs independent of audio — use sparingly, for self-contained visual statements.

name: transformElement
description: Animate the CSS transform of an existing element — rotate, scale, or translate it over duration_ms. Use when the music asks for motion of something already placed, not a new placement. A calligraphic stroke rotated into a new angle as the phrase arrives; a lantern scaled up as the music crests; a figure translated toward the edge as the music recedes. element_id comes from the scene state summary. Safe to call repeatedly on the same element — the last transform wins, the intermediate ones animate to it. Returns { ok: true } or an error tool result.

element_id.description: The element_id of the element to transform (e.g. "elem_0004"). Must match an id visible in the current scene state summary.

transform.description: At least one of rotate, scale, translate must be present.

rotate.description: Rotation in degrees. Positive = clockwise. E.g. 15, -30, 180.

scale.description: Uniform scale factor. 1.0 = no change; 1.4 = grow 40%; 0.6 = shrink.

translate.description: Translation in pixels relative to the element's current position.

duration_ms.description: How long the animation takes, in milliseconds. 300–1200 for a musical gesture; 2000+ for a slow drift. Must be >= 0.

name: morphElement
description: Cross-fade an existing element from its current asset to a new one of the same element_id. The element stays, but what it depicts changes. Use when one figurative form should become another mid-phrase — a palm tree that becomes a minaret as the melody rises to the ghammaz, a doorway that becomes a threshold of light as the cadence approaches, a single line of calligraphy that becomes a small lantern when the phrase resolves. element_id and duration_ms govern the overlap of the cross-fade.

element_id.description: The element_id of the element to morph (e.g. "elem_0004"). Must match an id visible in the current scene state summary.

to.description: The new asset. Either a new svg_markup (inline SVG), or a new image search query.

content_or_src.description: For type='svg': the full inline SVG markup of the destination form. For type='image': the search query for the destination image.

duration_ms.description: Cross-fade duration in milliseconds. 600–1600 for a legible morph; shorter reads as a cut, longer reads as a dissolve.

name: pulseScene
description: Flash the whole stage with a full-screen color overlay that fades out. Use for climactic or phrase-boundary emphasis — the moment the augmented second lands, the instant of a tahwil arrival, the final breath before a return to the tonic. Not a tool to reach for often; a single pulse per phrase is a strong gesture, and several in a row deaden the scene.

intensity.description: Peak opacity of the overlay, 0.0–1.0. 0.2 is a soft glow; 0.6 is a clear flush; 0.9 is a near-blinding wash that reads as rupture.

color.description: Optional CSS color for the overlay. Examples: "#d59c6a" (warm lantern), "#eadfce" (threshold light), "rgba(255,230,200,0.9)". Omit for a neutral warm amber.

duration_ms.description: Total duration of the flash + fade-out, in milliseconds. 300–900 for a gesture; 1500+ for a sustained wash.

name: paletteShift
description: Globally shift the stage's color temperature by applying a CSS filter over the root container. Use when the music moves into a different tonal world — a warm lantern-amber reading settling into a cool threshold-dusk reading as the upper jins opens, or a saturation drop across a held tonic. This re-colors everything already on screen; nothing is removed and nothing is re-placed. target specifies the shift in hue (degrees), saturation (multiplier), and lightness (multiplier). At least one of the three must be supplied.

target.description: The palette shift. At least one of hue, saturation, lightness must be present.

hue.description: Hue rotation in degrees. Positive shifts warm→cool in the typical range. E.g. -20 (warmer), 40 (cooler).

saturation.description: Saturation multiplier, 0.0–2.0. 1.0 = unchanged; 0.4 = washed-out; 1.6 = deepened. Must be >= 0.

lightness.description: Brightness multiplier, 0.0–2.0. 1.0 = unchanged; 0.7 = dimmer; 1.3 = brighter. Must be >= 0.

duration_ms.description: How long the shift takes, in milliseconds. 800–4000 for a musical transition; shorter reads as a jolt, longer as a drift.

name: textAnimate
description: Animate an already-placed text fragment. Use for gradual reveal, rhythmic emphasis, or anxious instability — not for decoration. effect is one of: "typewriter" (character-by-character reveal), "wordByWord" (word cascade with staggered fade-in), "marquee" (slow slide), "shake" (trembling displacement). element_id names a text element already on the scene. duration_ms is the full span of the animation.

element_id.description: The element_id of the text element to animate (e.g. "elem_0004"). Must match an id visible in the current scene state summary and that element must be of type 'text'.

effect.description: typewriter reveals characters one-at-a-time (good for testimonial phrases). wordByWord fades each word in sequence (good for measured arrivals). marquee slides the text horizontally (use sparingly — it draws the eye). shake trembles the text (for anxious or broken moments).

duration_ms.description: Animation span in milliseconds. 1500–4000 for a gradual reveal; 400–900 for a quick shake.

name: addP5Sketch
description: Place a localized p5.js sketch at a specific position. Used for gestural, short-lived visual responses — a lantern flickering, a hand drawing a line, a textile unfurling. Sketches MUST depict recognizable things. Up to 3 concurrent localized sketches; a 4th auto-retires the oldest (no rejection, you always get a sketch_id). Returns {sketch_id, retired_id?}.

position.description: One of the nine anchor positions.

size.description: Sketch canvas size. small ≈ 300×300, medium ≈ 500×500, large ≈ 800×800.

code.description: Complete p5.js sketch as JavaScript. See setP5Background for the window.features contract and the figurative-only constraint.

audio_reactive.description: If true, window.features updates every frame. If false, the sketch is self-contained.

lifetime_s.description: Optional lifetime in seconds. Omit for default permanence; supply a number for transient gestures that should retire automatically.

layer.description: Optional depth band for this localized sketch. Default is background (the sketch sits behind DOM elements). Pass midground or foreground only when the sketch should appear over images/text.
